matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: PCSK9 gain-of-function model (PCSK9 familial hypercholesterolemia gain-of-function model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PCSK9 LDL-receptor-trafficking thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL-C set point, hepatic LDL receptor recycling/degradation, cardiovascular lipid traits, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or PCSK9 inhibitor target biology.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs11591147 (PCSK9 R46L) dosage suggests this sample carries the PCSK9 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a PCSK9 LDL-receptor-trafficking thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL-C set point, hepatic LDL receptor recycling/degradation, cardiovascular lipid traits, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or PCSK9 inhibitor target biology. This specific marker is curated as PCSK9 loss-of-function LDL-C marker. rs11591147 (PCSK9 R46L) is curated as a PCSK9 marker for LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk biology, Familial hypercholesterolemia review context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the LDL receptor trafficking, hepatic cholesterol homeostasis, secreted protease biology, and LDL-C regulation pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs11591147 (PCSK9 R46L) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the PCSK9 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag LDL receptor trafficking, hepatic cholesterol homeostasis, secreted protease biology, and LDL-C regulation.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PCSK9 LDL-receptor-trafficking thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL-C set point, hepatic LDL receptor recycling/degradation, cardiovascular lipid traits, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or PCSK9 inhibitor target biology. An observed PCSK9 variant places this sample in the curated PCSK9 research context. The local PCSK9 bundle is lipid and pharmacogenetic-mechanism oriented. Loss-of-function and gain-of-function alleles can have different LDL-C and cardiovascular-risk implications. PCSK9 loss-of-function alleles tend to lower LDL-C through increased LDL receptor availability.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk biology

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a PCSK9 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PCSK9 LDL-receptor-trafficking thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL-C set point, hepatic LDL receptor recycling/degradation, cardiovascular lipid traits, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or PCSK9 inhibitor target biology. PCSK9 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a LDL receptor trafficking, hepatic cholesterol homeostasis, secreted protease biology, and LDL-C regulation gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local PCSK9 bundle is lipid and pharmacogenetic-mechanism oriented. Loss-of-function and gain-of-function alleles can have different LDL-C and cardiovascular-risk implications. PCSK9 loss-of-function alleles tend to lower LDL-C through increased LDL receptor availability.
